summary: Stunning Miller SBD capture (with minor clipping and pitch quirks) documents a solid early‑'90 show highlighted by a brilliant Greatest Story opener, classic Truckin'>Spoonful and Brent’s peak keyboard work on Take You Home.
review: Both reviewers praise the recording's overall fidelity – one calling it “stunning quality” and another noting the Miller SBD’s crispness despite some clipped intros and a slight speed issue that needs pitch correction. Musically, the show is described as a strong middle night of the 1990 run, featuring standout moments like Greatest Story, a classic Truckin'>Spoonful, and Brent’s superb Take You Home, while a few segments (Around & Around, Drums>Space) fall flat.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed reviews give generally positive comments on performance and recording but note several technical flaws and a few weak song moments, leading to an overall moderate‑high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the Miller SBD as "stunning" and very clean, but note clipped beginnings of Eyes, Wang Dang and Masterpiece, a slight fast running speed requiring about -1% pitch correction, and occasional drop‑outs.

standout_songs: Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: The middle show of a strong early‑'90 run flows well, with a purposeful first set and a second set that builds momentum despite missing the opening of Eyes and a few clipped intros.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s playing on Around & Around is criticized as out of place, and his guitar work is noted as weaker compared to other releases.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard performance is repeatedly praised – highlighted as his best take of Take You Home and a driving force under Samson.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drums>Space segment is described as less impressive than elsewhere on the tour.
